water.title: Water Intake Calculator
water.description: Calculate how much water you should drink daily based on your weight, activity, and climate.
water.tip: Spread your intake throughout the day for best hydration.

faq.q: How much water should I drink daily?
faq.a: EFSA recommends about 2.0 L/day for women and 2.5 L/day for men from beverages, with additional water from food. Needs rise with exercise, heat, and breastfeeding.

faq.q: How is personal water need calculated?
faq.a: A common rule of thumb is 30–35 ml per kg of body weight. Add 0.5–1 L per hour of intense exercise or hot-weather exposure.

faq.q: Do coffee and tea count toward fluid intake?
faq.a: Yes. Recent EFSA reviews show moderate caffeine intake does not cause net dehydration. Water, unsweetened teas, and diluted juices all contribute.

faq.q: What are the signs of dehydration?
faq.a: Dark urine, thirst, headaches, fatigue, and reduced concentration. Light straw-colored urine is a good hydration indicator.

faq.q: Can you drink too much water?
faq.a: Yes, in rare cases excessive intake (several liters in a short time) can cause hyponatremia. Spreading intake throughout the day is safe.
Yes. Intense exercise costs roughly 0.5–1 L of sweat per hour. For prolonged activity, electrolytes (sodium, potassium) should be added.
